title: Tragacantha leucophylla
taxonomy: Tragacantha leucophylla is classified as a type of shrub or subshrub in the pea family (Fabaceae). It belongs to the genus Crotalaria, although some sources may still list it under the older name Tragacantha. The genus Crotalaria is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. Tragacantha leucophylla is often considered a synonym for Crotalaria leucantha, which is a species of flowering plant. Further research is needed to determine the exact classification and nomenclature of this plant.

intro: Tragacantha leucophylla, also known as Astragalus leucophyllus, is a type of flowering plant in the legume family, commonly found in the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues and other illnesses. The bioactive constituents of Tragacantha leucophylla include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tragacantha leucophylla is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being dried and used in capsule or powder form.
